headline: Sir Hiccup McCough Breaks World Record For Longest Hiccup, Lasting 47 Years
summary: He finally stopped when someone said 'boo' from behind unexpectedly.
fullBody: Sir Hiccup McCough has officially broken the world record for the longest continuous hiccup, lasting 47 years. 'Hic—I'm so proud,' he said. 'Hic—It's been a journey.' The hiccups finally stopped when his granddaughter snuck up behind him and yelled 'boo.' 'I forgot what silence sounded like,' McCough whispered. Guinness World Records is sending a certificate, though McCough admits he 'still gets the occasional hiccup when nervous.'
